sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Hellas Verona, currently sitting at 16th in the league with 32 points, will host Cagliari, who are slightly ahead in 14th place with 33 points, in Round 34 at the Stadio Marcantonio Bentegodi in Verona. This matchup promises to be a tight contest between two mid-table teams fighting for crucial points. With both sides separated by just one point, the stakes are high as they look to avoid the relegation zone. The referee, Rosario Abisso, is known for a high disciplinary average, dishing out 5.4 yellow cards and 0.36 red cards per game, which could influence the flow of this encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last matchup on November 29, 2024, Cagliari edged out Hellas Verona with a narrow 1-0 victory at home. Tommaso Augello was the standout performer in that game, earning a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Over the last 5 meetings between these teams, Cagliari has won 2, Hellas Verona has secured 1 win, and 2 matches have ended in draws, indicating a closely contested history. Notably, 6 out of the last 7 head-to-head games have seen less than 2.5 goals, suggesting we might be in for another low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Hellas Verona are in a worrying run of form, currently on a streak of 4 games without a win, which they’ll be desperate to break at home. Cagliari, on the other hand, have also struggled recently, though their streak of 3 games without a win has ended. Both teams have shown a tendency for low-scoring games, with Verona’s last 5 matches all having less than 2.5 goals. In terms of discipline, Verona’s games often see fewer than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 matches, while Cagliari’s last 10 games have consistently stayed under this mark. Corner stats also suggest caution, as Verona’s last 8 matches and 7 of Cagliari’s last 9 have had fewer than 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players to Watch
sections.text: For Hellas Verona, Nicolás Valentini stands out with an impressive league rank of 3 in aerial duels won at 78.7% and long balls in total passes at 9.6%, making him a key defensive asset. Up front, Daniel Mosquera has contributed 5 goals from an expected goals (xG) of 6.34, showing decent output despite underperforming by -1.34. For Cagliari, Tommaso Augello is a major threat from the back, ranking 2nd in the league for big chances created with 12 and providing 6 assists. Nadir Zortea has been clinical with 6 goals from an xG of 3.00, overperforming significantly by 2.9981. Keep an eye on Nicolas Viola as well, who has a history of performing well against Verona with an average Sofascore rating of 7.3 over the last 5 matchups, despite not scoring.

sections.subtitle: Other Key Contributors
sections.text: Hellas Verona will also rely on players like Ondrej Duda, who has a solid Sofascore rating of 6.96 and has chipped in with 1 goal and 2 assists. Amin Sarr, another forward, has overperformed his xG by 1.4327, scoring 4 goals from an expected 2.57. On Cagliari’s side, Răzvan Marin offers creativity in midfield with 3 goals and 2 assists, alongside a Sofascore rating of 6.78. Gianluca Gaetano has also been influential with 4 assists, though he’s underperformed his xG of 4.84 by -2.8423, managing only 2 goals.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style
sections.text: Hellas Verona have struggled offensively, scoring 30 goals this season (15th in the league) while conceding 63 (19th for least goals conceded). They excel defensively in tackles per game at 17.63 (3rd in the league) and interceptions per game at 8.74 (1st), but their possession game is poor at 38.54% (20th). Cagliari’s goal distribution shows strength in the latter stages of matches, with 10 goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes, representing 11.76% of their total. Verona, however, peak early with 25.4% of their goals coming between the 16th and 30th minutes, which could set up an intriguing tactical battle.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Hellas Verona will miss forward Casper Tengstedt, who has scored 6 goals from an xG of 5.16, a significant loss to their attack. Defender Paweł Dawidowicz is also absent, potentially weakening their backline. Cagliari, meanwhile, will be without defender Yerry Mina, who has a Sofascore rating of 7.02, which could impact their defensive stability. These absences might tilt the balance slightly, depending on how each team adapts.
